words.def: a plan or system for doing or organizing something
words.example: a training scheme

words.def: a person who knows a lot about a particular subject because they have studied it in detail
words.example: a classical scholar

words.def: an amount of money given to somebody by an organization to help pay for their education
words.example: She won a scholarship to study at Stanford.

words.def: a mark, a cut or an injury made by scratching somebody’s skin or the surface of something
words.example: Her hands were covered in scratches from the brambles.

words.def: a loud high shout made by somebody who is hurt, frightened, excited, etc.; a loud high noise
words.example: scream of something, She let out a scream of pain.

words.def: the flat surface at the front of a television, computer, or other electronic device, on which you see pictures or information
words.example: a computer screen

words.def: the act of showing a film or television programme
words.example: This will be the movie's first screening in this country.

words.def: a place where you can sit, for example a chair
words.example: She sat back in her seat.

words.def: connected with teaching children of 11–18 years
words.example: secondary teachers

words.def: used to introduce the second of a list of points you want to make in a speech or piece of writing
words.example: Firstly, it's expensive, and secondly, it's too slow.

words.def: a part of an area of activity, especially of a country’s economy
words.example: the service/banking/manufacturing/financial sector

words.def: likely to continue or be successful for a long time
words.example: a secure job/income

words.def: to look for something/somebody
words.example: seek something/somebody, Drivers are advised to seek alternative routes.

words.def: a person who is trying to find or get the thing mentioned
words.example: an attention/a publicity seeker

words.def: to choose somebody/something from a group of people or things, usually according to a system
words.example: select somebody/something for something, He hasn't been selected for the team.

words.def: the process of choosing somebody/something from a group of people or things, usually according to a system
words.example: She took a long time to make her selection.

words.def: the type of person you are, especially the way you normally behave, look or feel
words.example: You'll soon be feeling your old self again (= feeling well or happy again).

words.def: a class at a university or college when a small group of students and a teacher discuss or study a particular topic
words.example: Teaching is by lectures and seminars.

words.def: high in rank or status; higher in rank or status than others
words.example: a senior official/officer/manager/executive

words.def: an understanding about something; an ability to judge something
words.example: One of the most important things in a partner is a sense of humour (= the ability to find things funny or make people laugh).

words.def: able to make good judgements based on reason and experience rather than emotion; practical
words.example: She's a sensible sort of person.

words.def: aware of and able to understand other people and their feelings
words.example: a sensitive and caring man

words.def: a set of words expressing a statement, a question or an order, usually containing a subject and a verb. In written English sentences begin with a capital letter and end with a full stop/period (.), a question mark (?) or an exclamation mark (!).
words.example: Does the sentence contain an adverb?

words.def: a set of events, actions, numbers, etc. which have a particular order and which lead to a particular result
words.example: He described the sequence of events leading up to the robbery.

words.def: a person who works in another person’s house, and cooks, cleans, etc. for them
words.example: a domestic servant

words.def: a period of time that is spent doing a particular activity
words.example: a training/practice session

words.def: to put an end to an argument or a disagreement
words.example: settle something, to settle a dispute/a matter/an issue

words.def: a person who goes to live in a new country or region
words.example: Most of the settlers came from England.

words.def: extremely bad or serious
words.example: His injuries are severe.

words.def: very badly or seriously
words.example: severely injured

words.def: connected with the physical activity of sex
words.example: sexual behaviour/activity/desire

words.def: sexually attractive
words.example: the sexy lead singer

words.def: an area that is dark and cool under or behind something, for example a tree or building, because the sun’s light does not get to it
words.example: shade of something, The shade of the pine tree provided some protection from the sun.

words.def: the dark shape that somebody/something’s form makes on a surface, for example on the ground, when they are between the light and the surface
words.example: The children were having fun, chasing each other's shadows.

words.def: not having much distance between the top or surface and the bottom
words.example: a shallow dish/pan/bowl

words.def: used to say that something is a cause for feeling sad or disappointed
words.example: She's retiring because of ill health, which is a great shame.

words.def: the form of the outer edges or surfaces of something; an example of something that has a particular form
words.example: a rectangular/spherical/cylindrical shape

words.def: having the type of shape mentioned
words.example: a huge balloon shaped like a giant cow

words.def: the fact of having a place to live or stay, considered as a basic human need
words.example: Human beings need food, clothing and shelter.

words.def: a period of time worked by a group of workers who start work as another group finishes
words.example: to work a shift

words.def: a large boat that carries people or goods by sea
words.example: The yard builds ships for the Navy.

words.def: a strong feeling of surprise as a result of something happening, especially something unpleasant; the event that causes this feeling
words.example: I got a terrible shock the other day.

words.def: surprised and upset; showing that somebody feels surprised and upset
words.example: I was quite shocked when I found out what he'd done.

words.def: that offends or upsets people; that is morally wrong
words.example: shocking behaviour

words.def: a situation in which a person is shot with a gun
words.example: Terrorist groups claimed responsibility for the shootings and bomb attacks.

words.def: the land along the edge of the sea, the ocean or a lake
words.example: a rocky/sandy shore

words.def: lasting a short time; designed only for a short period of time in the future
words.example: a short-term loan

words.def: a situation when there is not enough of the people or things that are needed
words.example: food/housing/water shortages

words.def: a short time; not long
words.example: She arrived shortly after us.

words.def: the act of firing a gun; the sound this makes
words.example: We heard some shots in the distance.

words.def: a brother or sister
words.example: squabbles between siblings

words.def: your name as you usually write it, for example at the end of a letter
words.example: Someone had forged her signature on the cheque.

words.def: the importance of something, especially when this has an effect on what happens in the future
words.example: a decision of major political significance

words.def: large or important enough to have an effect or to be noticed
words.example: There are no significant differences between the two groups of students.

words.def: in a way that is large or important enough to have an effect on something or to be noticed
words.example: significantly higher/lower/larger/smaller

words.def: a complete lack of noise or sound
words.example: Their footsteps echoed in the silence.

words.def: fine soft thread produced by silkworms
words.example: The caterpillar spins the silk around its entire body.

words.def: from a time in the past until a later past time, or until now
words.example: She went for a run on Monday and has not been seen since.

words.def: showing what you really think or feel
words.example: a sincere apology

words.def: having enough ability, experience and knowledge to be able to do something well
words.example: a skilled engineer/negotiator/craftsman

words.def: the bone structure that forms the head and surrounds and protects the brain
words.example: a fractured skull

words.def: a person who is owned by another person and is forced to work for and obey them
words.example: A former slave, he graduated from Clafflin University in South Carolina.

words.def: one page of an electronic presentation, that may contain text and images, that is usually viewed on a computer screen or projected onto a larger screen
words.example: Here's a slide showing target markets.

words.def: very small in degree
words.example: a slight increase/change/difference

words.def: to slide a short distance by accident so that you fall or nearly fall
words.example: She slipped and landed flat on her back.
